description: Whether MCP server configuration supports environment variable expansion. Reduces the need for hardcoded secrets in config files.

amp.mechanism: Amp MCP configuration does not document environment variable expansion

claude-code.mechanism: mcp_env_var_expansion: ${VAR} and ${VAR:-default} syntax in command, args, env, url, and headers fields of .mcp.json

cline.mechanism: Cline MCP configuration does not document environment variable expansion syntax

codex.mechanism: mcp_env_vars: Codex RawMcpServerConfig supports an env_vars array of McpServerEnvVar entries; each entry is either a plain string or a structured object with name and optional source fields enabling named environment variable sourcing

copilot-cli.mechanism: Copilot CLI MCP configuration does not document environment variable expansion

crush.mechanism: Crush MCP configuration supports environment variable references in server env blocks

cursor.mechanism: Server configuration supports variable interpolation using ${env:NAME} for environment variables, ${userHome} for the user's home directory, and ${workspaceFolder} for the workspace root — not limited to env blocks alone.

factory-droid.mechanism: stdio server add command accepts --env KEY=VALUE flags; env field documented in configuration schema for stdio servers

gemini-cli.mechanism: env_variable_expansion: Gemini CLI MCP configuration supports environment variable expansion for secrets and paths

kiro.mechanism: kiro_mcp_env_var_expansion: Environment variables referenced dynamically using ${VARIABLE_NAME} syntax within MCP config values; recommended pattern for secrets instead of hardcoded values

opencode.mechanism: Remote MCP server headers and OAuth fields support {env:VAR_NAME} syntax for environment variable interpolation; local servers accept an environment object with key-value pairs

roo-code.mechanism: env_variable_expansion: server env blocks accept environment variables so secrets can be referenced from the shell environment

windsurf.mechanism: config_interpolation: Windsurf MCP configuration supports ${env:VAR_NAME} and ${file:/path} interpolation for environment variables and file contents

zed.mechanism: no automatic environment variable expansion documented in Zed MCP env blocks
